The infection caused by Schistosoma mansoni is linked to drugs including Praziquantel, Oxamniquine, Artemether, and Acetylcysteine, with triglycerides noted as playing a role in the disease's pathogenesis. Additional drugs associated with various infections include Benzylpenicillin, Cyclophosphamide, Methotrexate, and Vancomycin, among others.
182: An outbreak of Visceral Leishmaniasis occurred in Pakistan on January 16, 2002, with 738 confirmed cases, followed by two additional outbreaks on May 22 and June 28, 2002, each reporting 200,000 confirmed cases. Visceral Leishmaniasis is classified as a type of leishmaniasis, which in turn is a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. The condition is associated with a broad range of drugs, including Amphotericin B, Meglumine antimoniate, Miltefosine, Pentamidine, Antimony, and its derivatives such as Antimony Sodium Gluconate and Antimony potassium tartrate. Other drugs linked to treatment or study include Allopurinol, Artemisinin, Isosorbide dinitrate, Ketoconazole, Paromomycin, and various organotin compounds, chromans, and plant-derived extracts. The disease is also associated with the Leishmaniasis pathway. Additionally, Visceral Leishmaniasis shares a relationship with cancer, as both are categorized under diseases of cellular proliferation, though no direct pathogenic role of cancer-related drugs in Visceral Leishmaniasis is specified.
183:
Outbreaks of Zika virus have been reported across multiple countries, including El Salvador, Guatemala, Ecuador, Republic of Panama, Mexico, Papua New Guinea, The Netherlands, St Lucia, United States, Viet Nam, French Guiana, India, and others, with case counts ranging from 1 to 8,750. Notable events include a large outbreak in French Guiana in November 2015 with 8,750 cases, and additional outbreaks in 2016 in the United States, Ecuador, and Panama. The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease. Pharmacological agents associated with Zika virus include Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, IDX184, and Niclosamide.

Outbreaks of Dengue Fever have occurred in numerous regions, including Malaysia, Sri Lanka, Brazil, India, Cameroon, East Timor, Barbados, The Netherlands, and others, with case numbers ranging from 1 to over 80,000. Severe outbreaks were reported in Sri Lanka in 2017 with 80,732 cases and 215 deaths, and in Brazil in 2002 with 317,787 cases. The disease is also classified as a viral infectious disease. It is associated with a range of drugs, including 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, Doxycycline, F2-Isoprostanes, Hydroxychloroquine, Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic Acids, Rolitetracycline, Silver, cholest-5-en-3 beta,7 alpha-diol, hinokiflavone, and podocarpusflavone A. Dengue hemorrhagic fever is recognized as a subtype of dengue disease.

Malaria outbreaks have been documented in the United Republic of Tanzania, Burundi, Ethiopia, and Pakistan, with a particularly large outbreak in Pakistan in 2022 involving 3,400,000 cases. The disease is classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. Symptoms include fever, chills, diarrhea, vomiting, jaundice, coma, unconsciousness, retinal hemorrhage, purpura, oliguria, pallor, pruritus, and others. Drugs associated with malaria treatment include Amodiaquine, Artemether, Artemisinin, Artesunate, Atovaquone, Chloroquine, Doxycycline, Halofantrine, Hydroxychloroquine, Mefloquine, Primaquine, Pyrimethamine, Quinine, and various combinations such as atovaquone-proguanil and amodiaquine-artesunate. The disease is also linked to the Malaria pathway, and its pathogenesis involves cholesterol, glutamic acid, and bilirubin.

Outbreaks of Chikungunya virus disease have been reported in the United Republic of Tanzania, the United States, Congo, Cameroon, France, Reunion, Italy, Spain, Argentina, Egypt, and India, with case counts ranging from 1 to over 27,000. A significant outbreak occurred in Chad in 2020 with 27,540 cases and one death. The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease. Drugs associated with Chikungunya include Chloroquine.
184:
An outbreak of pneumonia was reported in Vietnam and Indonesia on March 12, 2003, with 305 confirmed cases and 5 deaths. Pneumonia is classified as a lung disease and is associated with a range of drugs, including amoxicillin-potassium clavulanate combination, azithromycin, doxycycline, and amphotericin B. Additionally, arginine has been identified as playing a role in the disease's pathogenesis. 

Visceral leishmaniasis has been documented in Pakistan across multiple events in 2002 and 2002, with case counts ranging from 738 to 200,000. This condition is a subtype of leishmaniasis, which itself is categorized as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. Treatment options include amphotericin B, antimony compounds, meglumine antimoniate, miltefosine, pentamidine, and various other antiprotozoals. The disease is also associated with several experimental compounds and natural extracts.

Malaria has been reported in multiple locations and time periods, including Burundi in 1999 with 616,034 cases and 51 deaths, the United Republic of Tanzania in 1998 and 1999, Ethiopia in 2024, and Pakistan in 2022 with 3,400,000 cases. Malaria is also classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. It presents with symptoms such as fever, chills, jaundice, coma, diarrhea, vomiting, and neurological manifestations. The disease is linked to several drugs, including chloroquine, artemisinin, quinine, and atovaquone-proguanil combination, with cholesterol, glutamic acid, and bilirubin implicated in its pathogenesis. The condition is associated with the malaria pathway and is connected to hepatitis B and hepatitis C in terms of biological pathways. 

Leishmaniasis is a parasitic protozoa infectious disease and is associated with a broad range of drugs, including amphotericin B, antimony compounds, miltefosine, and various other antiprotozoals. The disease is linked to the leishmaniasis pathway. Cutaneous leishmaniasis, a related form, shares similar drug associations and is also classified under leishmaniasis. Hypersensitivity reaction type IV disease is associated with drugs such as lipopolysaccharides, mercury, and toluene 2,4-diisocyanate, and is categorized as a hypersensitivity reaction disease. Hepatocellular carcinoma is a subtype of liver carcinoma and is associated with pathways including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, microRNAs in cancer, and viral carcinogenesis. It is linked to drugs such as methotrexate, tamibarotene, and chloroquine diphosphate, with aspartic acid, acylcarnitine, and tumor-associated antigen 72 implicated in its pathogenesis.
185: Elephantiasis is classified as a type of lymphedema. It is associated with the drugs quartz, smectite, and mica. Filarial elephantiasis is a subtype of filariasis and is treated with albendazole, diethylcarbamazine, and ivermectin. Lymphedema, in turn, is categorized as a lymphatic system disease and is associated with the drugs melphalan and sirolimus; caprolactone plays a role in its disease pathogenesis. Biological pathways linked to lymphedema include AKT phosphorylation of nuclear targets, focal adhesion, RAF/MAP kinase cascade, and signaling to p38 via RIT and RIN. Filariasis is a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with acetylsalicylic acid and levamisole.
186: Hemorrhage is associated with a wide range of drugs, including acetylsalicylic acid, aconitine, amifostine, anticonvulsants, BUCY-2 protocol, beraprost, butylated hydroxytoluene, cadmium chloride, carbimazole, celecoxib, cocaine, contraceptives, oral hormonal, cyclophosphamide, danaparoid, danazol, diatrizoate, edetic acid, endosulfan, fenoldopam, heparin calcium, lepirudin, lipopolysaccharides, methimazole, midomafetamine, mifepristone, misoprostol, monocrotophos, netilmicin, nicorandil, olmesartan medoxomil, oxothiazolidinecarboxylic acid, particulate matter, penicillamine, phenytoin, propylthiouracil, rosiglitazone, ruthenium red, simvastatin, succinylcholine, telmisartan, thalidomide, theophylline, trinitrobenzenesulfonic acid, vasopressins, ankaferd blood stopper, antivitamins K, arginine 2-mercaptoethane sulfonate, chloroacetaldehyde, tcdA protein from Clostridium difficile, and tris(chloroethyl)phosphate. Thrombocytopenia, a condition characterized by low platelet count, is linked to drugs such as 1-arabinofuranosylcytosine-5'-stearylphosphate, 5-FU-CDDP protocol, acetylsalicylic acid, ajmaline, amiodarone, ampicillin, argatroban, auranofin, benzene, bortezomib, CHOP protocol, carbimazole, cyclosporine, dacarbazine, dactinomycin, dioxins, diquat, doxepin, epirubicin, everolimus, fenretinide, gold, heme, indomethacin, isotretinoin, lenalidomide, lipopolysaccharides, mefenamic acid, methimazole, methotrexate, mycophenolic acid, navitoclax, nedaplatin, peginterferon alfa-2b, penicillamine, pentosan sulfuric polyester, phenytoin, pyrazinamide, sirolimus, TP protocol, tamoxifen, tazobactam and piperacillin, terbinafine, tioguanine, valganciclovir, warfarin, zorubicin, estrogens, interferons, and liposomal doxorubicin. Additionally, aspartic acid is implicated in the pathogenesis of thrombocytopenia. The condition is associated with the tight junction pathway. Thrombocytopenia is classified as a blood platelet disease. Disseminated intravascular coagulation is linked to drugs such as acenocoumarol, acetylcysteine, acetylsalicylic acid, alprazolam, aminocaproic acid, amphetamine, atorvastatin, cefotaxime, cefoxitin, cladribine, contrast media, dexamethasone, diamorphine, diethylstilbestrol diphosphate, disopyramide, dronabinol, edaravone, epinephrine, estradiol, estradiol congeners, griseofulvin, haloperidol, heparin, heparin, low-molecular-weight, hirudins, ibuprofen, indomethacin, isoniazid, lamotrigine, lepirudin, metamfetamine, methotrimeprazine, midomafetamine, olanzapine, particulate matter, penicillamine, phenmetrazine, piperacillin, progesterone, proguanil, pyrimethamine, rifampicin, scorpion venoms, succinylcholine, tegafur, thalidomide, tranexamic acid, warfarin, zomepirac, and estrogens. It is classified as a thrombophilia. Acute kidney failure is associated with drugs including 2-bromoethylamine, 4-benzyl-2-methyl-1,2,4-thiadiazolidine-3,5-dione, 5-FU-CDDP protocol, aminosalicylic acid, azathioprine, bezafibrate, butobarbital, cefaloridine, charcoal, chlorprothixene, cimetidine, clarithromycin, clofibrate, disopyramide, ditiocarb, doxorubicin, ibutilide, iodine, iohexol, iopanoic acid, iothalamic acid, ketorolac tromethamine, lansoprazole, lisinopril, losartan, metrizoic acid, paraquat, particulate matter, peginterferon alfa-2a, pemetrexed, phenazopyridine, pheniramine, phenylbutazone, promethazine, propylene glycol, pyrazoles, pyrroles, ritonavir, silibinin, tacrolimus, tranexamic acid, triamterene, trimetazidine, vancomycin, vitamin E, benzamide, coenzyme Q10, diethyl sulfoxide, naphthalene, and notoginsenoside R1. It is classified as a kidney failure. Cardiotoxicity is associated with drugs such as 7-mono-O-(beta-hydroxyethyl)rutoside, acetylsalicylic acid, anthracyclines, antineoplastic agents, immunological, arsenic trioxide, astemizole, axitinib, cadmium, carbaryl, carnitine, crocin, curcumin, doxorubicin, epigallocatechin gallate, febuxostat, gefitinib, hydrogen sulfide, INX 08189, idarubicin, methylene blue, mexiletine, midomafetamine, mitoxantrone, NSC 23766, nandrolone decanoate, nefazodone, patulin, plant extracts, plant preparations, propofol, quercetin, rosiglitazone, sarin, sibutramine, silibinin, sunitinib, tetrachlorodibenzodioxin, vandetanib, adriamycinol, azaspiracid, coenzyme Q10, daunorubicinol, naringin, perfluorooctanoic acid, phosphine, pristimerin, sodium arsenite, sodium bisulfide, soybean oil phospholipid emulsion, and triadimefon. Edema is associated with drugs including 1-(4-(benzothiazol-2-yloxy)benzyl)piperidine-4-carboxylic acid, 1-(6-((3-methoxyestra-1,3,5(10)-trien-17-yl)amino)hexyl)-1H-pyrrole-2,5-dione, 2,3-dihydroxynaphthalene, 4,5-dihydro-1-(3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)-1H-pyrazol-3-amine, acitretin, antrafenine, atenolol, azathioprine, berberine, bumetanide, camphor, carbamazepine, carrageenan, cinnamaldehyde, cisplatin, dacarbazine, dextran sulfate, diazoxide, edotreotide, epigallocatechin gallate, erlotinib hydrochloride, exenatide, fucoxanthin, geraniol, heparin, iodine, lipopolysaccharides, mechlorethamine, metolazone, mibefradil, morphine, olmesartan medoxomil, propylthiouracil, resveratrol, ritanserin, sodium fluoride, tetracaine, thioacetamide, verapamil, ZK 216348, alpha-naphthoflavone, citronellal, citronellol, estragole, glycyrol, goniothalamin, hibifolin, hydroperoxyisophosphamide, lipopolysaccharide from Escherichia coli O111 B4, and sodium arsenite. Necrosis is associated with drugs such as 1-bromopropane, 2-nonenal, acetic acid, acetylsalicylic acid, alpha-amanitin, aluminum chloride, angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors, atorvastatin, benzo(a)pyrene, cediranib, d-glucose, etacrynic acid, flame retardants, ibuprofen, iproniazid, isoprenaline, lidocaine, lindane, lovastatin, methoxamine, methyl salicylate, mibefradil, morphine, N-(2(R)-2-(hydroxamidocarbonylmethyl)-4-methylpentanoyl)-L-tryptophan methylamide, N-(oxo-5,6-dihydrophenanthridin-2-yl)-N,N-dimethylacetamide hydrochloride, norfloxacin, phenobarbital, phenytoin, probenecid, quinolinic acid, ranitidine, SB 203580, sulfasalazine, taurine, tetrandrine, thiamethoxam, thiopental, trastuzumab emtansine, troglitazone, trolnitrate, valinomycin, cerous chloride, chlorozotocin, desethylamiodarone, dihydrocapsaicin, dioscin, enniatins, evodiamine, manumycin, monomethylarsonous acid, and is implicated in the pathogenesis of necrosis through magnesium.
187: An outbreak of Visceral Leishmaniasis occurred in Pakistan on January 16, 2002, with 738 confirmed cases, followed by two additional outbreaks on May 22 and June 28, 2002, each reporting 200,000 confirmed cases. Visceral Leishmaniasis is classified as a type of leishmaniasis, which in turn belongs to the broader category of parasitic protozoa infectious diseases. A range of drugs are associated with the treatment or study of Visceral Leishmaniasis, including Amphotericin B, Meglumine antimoniate, Miltefosine, Pentamidine, Antimony, and its derivatives such as Antimony Sodium Gluconate and Antimony potassium tartrate. Other compounds associated with the disease include Allopurinol, Artemisinin, Isosorbide dinitrate, Ketoconazole, Paromomycin, and various natural extracts such as Desmodium gangeticum extract and amarogentin. Additionally, several experimental and investigational agents are linked to the disease, including 2,5-bis(2-(2-propoxy)-4-(2-pyridylimino)aminophenyl)furan, 2-(2''-dichloroacetamidobenzyl)-3-(3'-indolylquinoline), and various chromans, organotin compounds, and nitric oxide-related agents. The disease is also associated with the Leishmaniasis pathway, and certain substances such as Homocysteine, Isoprostanes, Mannitol, Tocopherol, and Ubidecarenone are implicated in its pathogenesis.
188:
Chagas disease is classified as a type of trypanosomiasis. It is associated with several drugs, including 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-methoxypropyloxy)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, Benznidazole, N-pip-phenylalanine-homophenylalanine-vinyl sulfone phenyl, Nifurtimox, and Nitrofural. The disease is also linked to the biological pathway associated with Chagas disease.

Asthma is categorized as a bronchial disease and shares characteristics with atopic dermatitis and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. It presents with a range of symptoms, including cough, dyspnea, chest pain, respiratory sounds, sleep apnea, sneezing, snoring, and voice disorders. Several drugs are associated with asthma, including acetaminophen, salbutamol, salmeterol xinafoate, montelukast, and dexamethasone. Some drugs, such as 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, formaldehyde, and toluene 2,4-diisocyanate, are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis. The condition is also associated with the asthma pathway.

Hookworm disease is treated with albendazole, dichlorvos, ivermectin, oxantel pamoate, and tribendimidine.

Filariasis is classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with acetylsalicylic acid and levamisole.

Strongyloidiasis is also classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is treated with abamectin, dichlorvos, levamisole, metrifonate, and thiabendazole.

Tuberculosis is classified as a primary bacterial infectious disease. It is associated with a wide range of drugs, including isoniazid, rifampicin, pyrazinamide, ethambutol, streptomycin, and bedaquiline. Some drugs, such as lopinavir, phosphorus, and phosphates, are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis. The condition is linked to the tuberculosis pathway.

Infection is associated with various drugs, including benzylpenicillin, ciprofloxacin, dexamethasone, vancomycin, and cyclophosphamide.

Hypertension is classified as an artery disease and shares characteristics with abdominal obesity-metabolic syndrome 1, obesity, and type 2 diabetes mellitus. It presents with symptoms such as headache, dizziness, edema, proteinuria, and sleep apnea. Several drugs are associated with hypertension, including enalaprilat, ramipril, losartan, and methotrexate. Some drugs, such as 1-naphthaleneacetic acid, cotinine, and prostaglandins A, are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis.

Pneumoconiosis is classified as an interstitial lung disease and is associated with occupational air pollutants, coal dust, silica, and vehicle emissions.

COVID-19 is classified as a coronavirus infectious disease. It is associated with a range of drugs, including remdesivir, lopinavir, ritonavir, hydroxychloroquine, chloroquine, baricitinib, and ivermectin. Antiviral agents such as sofosbuvir, ribavirin, and umifenovir are also associated with the condition.

Cancer is classified as a disease of cellular proliferation. It is associated with drugs such as fluorouracil, cisplatin, methotrexate, and sorafenib. Some substances, including amines, glycine, and kynurenic acid, are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis.

Intestinal parasitism NOS is treated with abamectin, famphur, levamisole, oxfendazole, oxibendazole, praziquantel, pyrantel pamoate, and thiabendazole.

Toxocariasis is classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is treated with albendazole and prednisolone.

Schistosomiasis is treated with metrifonate and praziquantel.

Intestinal schistosomiasis is a subtype of schistosomiasis and is treated with praziquantel, quercetin, and resveratrol.
189: Outbreaks of Crimean-Congo Hemorrhagic Fever (CCHF) have been reported across multiple countries and time periods, including an event in South Africa (KwaZulu Natal) on November 5, 1996, with 32 confirmed cases and 1 death; a case in Egypt on March 11, 2003, involving 30 cases and 6 deaths; a large outbreak in Turkey on August 8, 2006, with 242 cases and 20 deaths; and a significant event in Iraq on May 22, 2022, reporting 212 cases and 27 deaths. Additional outbreaks occurred in Pakistan in 1998, 2000, 2010, and 1999, with case counts ranging from 4 to 65 and death tolls between 2 and 15. Kosovo experienced multiple outbreaks in 2001, with confirmed cases ranging from 44 to 69 and 4 to 6 deaths. The disease has also been reported in other regions without specific country details. Crimean-Congo Hemorrhagic Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease, and the drug Ribavirin is associated with its treatment or study.
190:
An outbreak of pneumonia occurred in Viet Nam and Indonesia on March 12, 2003, with 305 confirmed cases and 5 deaths. Pneumonia is classified as a lung disease and is associated with a range of drugs, including amoxicillin-potassium clavulanate combination, cefixime, ciprofloxacin, dapsone, gentamicins, and corticosteroids, among others. It is also linked to the pathogenesis of arginine. Additionally, several environmental and chemical agents such as asbestos, coal ash, formaldehyde, and particulate matter are associated with the condition.

Measles has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Egypt, Ecuador, Mexico, Ethiopia, Brazil, Madagascar, Somalia, Cook Islands, Central African Republic (North), Sudan (W Equatoria), Japan, the United States, Thailand, Africa, Europe, and the USA, with case counts ranging from 1 to over 413,000 and death tolls reaching up to 182. The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with the Measles pathway. The outbreaks have occurred across different time periods, indicating ongoing transmission in multiple countries.

Diphtheria outbreaks have been documented in Guinea, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Yemen, Federal Republic of Nigeria, and People's Republic of Bangladesh, with case numbers ranging from 12 to 804 and deaths from 2 to 73. The disease is categorized as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is associated with drugs such as daptomycin, imipenem, linezolid, rifampicin, tazobactam and piperacillin, telavancin, and quinupristin-dalfopristin.

Pertussis is classified as a commensal bacterial infectious disease and is associated with drugs including cefbuperazone, piperacillin, and tazobactam and piperacillin.

Tetanus is classified as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is associated with drugs such as baclofen, barbiturates, diazepam, labetalol, magnesium sulfate, midazolam, pancuronium, propofol, tetanus toxin, and vecuronium bromide.

Influenza is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with drugs such as amantadine, rimantadine, oseltamivir, oseltamivir acid, and various antivirals and immunomodulators. It is linked to the Influenza A pathway.

Tuberculosis is associated with drugs including isoniazid, rifampicin, pyrazinamide, ethambutol, streptomycin, and others, and is linked to the Tuberculosis pathway. It is also associated with the pathogenesis of lopinavir, phosphorus, and phosphates.

Respiratory tract diseases are associated with a wide range of drugs, including acetylsalicylic acid, air pollutants, carbon monoxide, formaldehyde, and various corticosteroids, and are linked to the pathogenesis of bilirubin.

Respiratory tract infections are associated with drugs such as amoxicillin-potassium clavulanate combination, cefixime, clarithromycin, doxycycline, and others, and are linked to the pathogenesis of clozapine.

Tonsillitis is classified as an upper respiratory tract disease and is associated with drugs such as cefbuperazone, cefixime, ceftriaxone, erythromycin, phenoxymethylpenicillin, and benzathine benzylpenicillin.
191:
Outbreaks of Zika virus have been reported across multiple countries between 2015 and 2021, including El Salvador, Guatemala, Ecuador, Republic of Panama, Mexico, Papua New Guinea, The Netherlands, St Lucia, United States, Viet Nam, French Guiana, Brazil, Argentina, Honduras, Dominican Republic, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Cape Verde, India, and others. Notable events include a large outbreak in French Guiana in November 2015 with 8,750 cases, and significant outbreaks in Brazil and Colombia in 2015–2016, with thousands of cases reported. Some outbreaks were associated with severe outcomes, such as deaths in Panama and the United States, and cases of congenital Zika syndrome have been linked to the virus. Zika fever is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with several drugs, including Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, IDX184, and Niclosamide, which are studied or used in conjunction with the condition.

Yellow Fever outbreaks have occurred in numerous countries, including Brazil, Cameroon, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Angola, Guinea Bissau, Ecuador, Congo, Mali, Egypt, Benin, Sudan, Ethiopia, and Tanzania, spanning from 1996 to 2022. Outbreaks have varied in scale, from small clusters with a few cases to large epidemics, such as the 2016 outbreak in Angola with 1,708 cases and 238 deaths, and the 2014 outbreak in the DRC with 139 cases and 6 deaths. Some outbreaks were associated with high mortality, including a 1996 outbreak in Benin with 86 cases and 65 deaths, and a 2005 outbreak in Guinea Bissau with 114 cases and 26 deaths. Yellow Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease and has been reported in multiple regions across Africa and South America, with recurring outbreaks in Brazil and Central Africa.
192:
An outbreak of diarrhoea occurred in Angola on May 26, 1998, reporting 560 confirmed cases and 12 deaths. 

Diarrhoea is classified as a gastrointestinal system disease. Several drugs are associated with diarrhoea, including Aconitine, Aliskiren, Alvimopan, Alvocidib, Amifostine, Amlodipine, Amodiaquine, Amoxicillin, Azacitidine, Benoxaprofen, Bortezomib, Bupropion, Carboplatin, Clomipramine, Copper, Cyclosporins, Daunorubicin, Dexamethasone, Dicoumarol, Doxorubicin, Felbamate, Iridoid Glycosides, LMWH-DOCA conjugate, Lansoprazole, Leflunomide, Letrozole, Lisinopril, MAV protocol, Meropenem, Metronidazole, Oxaliplatin, Paclitaxel docosahexaenoic acid, Pantoprazole, Pentoxifylline, Pinacidil, RN 1734, Racecadotril, SR 140333, Senna Extract, Solifenacin Succinate, Tacrolimus, Taribavirin, Tegaserod, Ticlopidine, Trimetrexate, Ursodeoxycholic acid, Valaciclovir, Valganciclovir, Vinblastine, and sangre de grado. Additionally, the drugs Alanine and Aspartic acid are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis, and Chlorides are associated with the condition. 

Hookworm disease is associated with the drugs Albendazole, Dichlorvos, Ivermectin, Oxantel pamoate, and tribendimidine.
193:
Cutaneous leishmaniasis is a form of leishmaniasis and is associated with a range of drugs including 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-methoxypropyloxy)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, allopurinol, amphotericin B, antimony, antimony sodium gluconate, antiprotozoals, gentamicins, indomethacin, itraconazole, ketoconazole, meglumine, meglumine antimoniate, miltefosine, omeprazole, paromomycin, pentamidine, pentoxifylline, sodium chloride, WR279396, zinc sulfate, liposomal amphotericin B, and mammea A-BB.

Mucocutaneous leishmaniasis is also classified as a type of leishmaniasis and is associated with amphotericin B, antimony, antimony sodium gluconate, antiprotozoals, meglumine antimoniate, pentamidine, and pentoxifylline.

Leishmaniasis is a parasitic protozoa infectious disease and is linked to multiple drugs such as 5,7-dihydroxy-8-(2-methylbutanoyl)-6-(3-methylbutyl)-4-phenyl-chroman-2-one, amphotericin B, antimony, antimony sodium gluconate, antiprotozoals, arsenic compounds, atovaquone, chromans, marbofloxacin, maytansine, meglumine antimoniate, miltefosine, N,N'-diphenyl-4-methoxybenzamidine, N-butyl-(1-(4-methoxy)phenyl-9H-beta-carboline)-3-carboxamide, nelfinavir, nitric oxide, pentamidine, tafenoquine, trifluralin, fenarimol, mammea A-BB, methyl gallate, oil of Chenopodium, and pistagremic acid. It is associated with the biological pathway known as Leishmaniasis.

Chickenpox is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with acetylsalicylic acid.

Fibrosis is associated with a broad range of substances and drugs, including 1-Naphthylisothiocyanate, AM 281, aldosterone, amiodarone, asbestos, amphibole, atorvastatin, biological products, bromocriptine, carbon tetrachloride, carnitine, clopidogrel, curcumin, dextropropoxyphene, diacetyl, dietary sucrose, diethylhexyl phthalate, diethylnitrosamine, etretinate, felodipine, geraniol, homocysteine, indomethacin, mercuric chloride, methapyrilene, mitomycin, nandrolone decanoate, nanotubes, carbon, nicorandil, octreotide, oxygen, pentazocine, perphenazine, pravastatin, sarpogrelate, Scutellaria baicalensis extract, streptozocin, sulforaphane, tamoxifen, taurine, telmisartan, tioguanine, trichostatin A, troglitazone, zingerone, zoledronic acid, fatostatin, furan, necrostatin-1, ochratoxin A, and perfluorooctane sulfonic acid.

Influenza is a viral infectious disease and is associated with 1'-methylspiro(adamantane-2,3'-pyrrolidine)maleate, acetylsalicylic acid, Active Hexose Correlated Compound, amantadine, azithromycin, camostat, isoquercitrin, masoprocol, oseltamivir, oseltamivir acid, ozone, probenecid, pseudoephedrine, quercetin, resveratrol, rimantadine, Ro 64-0802, selenium, sulfur dioxide, vitamin E, agathisflavone, amentoflavone, benzylsulfonylargininyl-proline-(4-amidinobenzyl)amide, pyrrolidine dithiocarbamic acid, and robustaflavone. It is associated with the biological pathway known as Influenza A.
194:
Outbreaks of Zika virus have been reported across multiple countries between 2015 and 2021, with notable events in French Guiana, Brazil, Ecuador, and the Republic of Panama, where case counts ranged from a few to over 8,000. Additional outbreaks occurred in El Salvador, Guatemala, Mexico, the United States, India, and several Caribbean and Pacific islands, including the Netherlands, St Lucia, and Papua New Guinea. Some outbreaks were associated with fatalities, such as in the Republic of Panama and El Salvador. The virus has also been documented in regions including Cape Verde, Honduras, Dominican Republic, and Trinidad and Tobago, with cases reported in both 2015 and 2016, and a resurgence in India in 2021.

West Nile Disease has been associated with multiple outbreaks, particularly in the United States and Canada during 2002, with significant case numbers and fatalities. In the United States, outbreaks in 2002 included over 3,500 confirmed cases and more than 200 deaths, while Canada reported hundreds of cases across several provinces during the same year. Earlier outbreaks were recorded in the Republic of Moldova in 1996, with over 400 cases and 39 deaths, and in Israel in 2000, with 151 cases and 12 deaths. Additional cases were reported in Brazil, Portugal, Romania, and Turkey, with the disease documented in various years, including 1999 and 2015. West Nile Disease is classified as a viral infectious disease. The drugs Active Hexose Correlated Compound and Minocycline are associated with this condition.
195: Dengue Fever has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Malaysia, where significant events occurred in 1996 and 1997 with over 2,000 and 11,000 cases respectively, and in Sri Lanka in 2017, which saw a large-scale outbreak with 80,732 cases and 215 deaths. Additional outbreaks have been documented in countries such as Brazil (2002), the Netherlands (2004), India (multiple years), Pakistan (2019 and 2021), Bangladesh (2022), Cameroon, El Salvador, Egypt, Thailand, and others, with case counts ranging from a few to over 58,000 in a single event. A particularly severe outbreak was reported in 2024, affecting an unspecified country with 7,600,000 confirmed cases and 3,000 deaths. The disease has also been recorded in East Timor, Barbados, France, Reunion (France), Uruguay, Ecuador, and several other locations, with varying case numbers and mortality rates. Dengue Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease and is a subtype of dengue disease, which itself is associated with dengue hemorrhagic fever. In terms of treatment, several drugs have been linked to the condition, including 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, doxycycline, hydroxychloroquine, hinokiflavone, podocarpusflavone A, and silver.
196:
Dengue Fever has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Malaysia, where significant events occurred in 1996 and 1997 with thousands of cases and dozens of deaths. Additional outbreaks were documented in Sri Lanka in 2017, with over 80,000 cases and 215 deaths, and in Brazil in 2002 and 2008, with over 300,000 and 120,000 cases respectively. Other notable outbreaks occurred in the Netherlands in 2004, with more than 58,000 cases and over 600 deaths, and in Pakistan in 2019, with over 47,000 cases and 75 deaths. Outbreaks have also been reported in countries such as Egypt, India, Thailand, the Philippines, Cameroon, Cape Verde, East Timor, and Reunion (France), with case counts ranging from a few to tens of thousands and death tolls varying from single digits to over 700. A large-scale outbreak was reported in 2024 with 7.6 million cases and 3,000 deaths, and another major outbreak in 2023 involved 500,000 cases and 750 deaths. The disease has also been observed in smaller outbreaks in countries including Spain, Portugal, France, Uruguay, and Ecuador, and in multiple countries across Asia, Africa, and South America in 1998.

Dengue Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease. It is also associated with a more severe form known as dengue hemorrhagic fever, which is considered a subtype of dengue disease. Several drugs have been observed to be associated with dengue disease, including 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, doxycycline, F2-isoprostanes, hydroxychloroquine, hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acids, rolitetracycline, silver, cholest-5-en-3 beta,7 alpha-diol, hinokiflavone, and podocarpusflavone A. Additionally, certain drugs such as pentazocine, vincristine, catecholamines, and chloroacetic acid are linked to iatrogenic disease, though their direct relationship to dengue is not specified.
197: Nipah Virus has been associated with multiple outbreaks across several countries, including Malaysia in 1999, the People's Republic of Bangladesh in 2001, 2004, and 2023, India in 2018 (with two separate events in May and July), and Pakistan in 2024. These outbreaks have varied in scale, with case counts ranging from 1 to 42 and death tolls from 1 to 17, indicating a high case fatality rate in some instances. The virus has demonstrated recurring transmission in South Asia, particularly in Bangladesh and India, with sustained or re-emerging activity over multiple years.
198: Yellow Fever has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various countries, including significant events in Angola, where large-scale outbreaks occurred in 2015 and 2016, with over 1,000 cases and more than 100 deaths in each instance. Additional outbreaks were documented in Nigeria, with a major event in 2017 involving 341 confirmed cases and 45 deaths. Other notable outbreaks occurred in Brazil, particularly in 2017, with over 700 cases and more than 200 deaths reported, and in Congo, where multiple outbreaks were recorded between 2009 and 2018, including one in 2016 with 151 cases and 21 deaths. Outbreaks have also been reported in Cameroon, Ecuador, Ghana, Mali, South Africa, French Guiana, and other regions, with case counts ranging from single cases to several hundred, and death tolls varying accordingly. The disease has been detected in countries across Africa, South America, and parts of Europe and Asia, indicating a wide geographic distribution. Yellow Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease. In terms of pharmacological associations, several drugs have been linked to the condition, including Acyclovir, Ganciclovir, Ribavirin, and Zidovudine, among others, though their specific roles in pathogenesis are not fully detailed. The disease is also associated with the TCR signaling pathway, and it falls under the broader category of brain diseases.
199:
Shigellosis has been associated with multiple outbreak events, including a significant occurrence in Sudan (W Equatoria) on July 14, 2004, with 1,340 confirmed cases and 11 deaths, another in Congo on November 5, 2003, involving 379 cases and 23 deaths, and a third in Cameroon on November 1, 1997, with 237 cases and 60 deaths. Shigellosis is classified as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is associated with the drugs Ampicillin and Nalidixic acid. The condition is linked to the Shigellosis pathway, indicating its biological relevance in disease mechanisms.

Diarrhoea, which has been reported in an outbreak in Angola on May 26, 1998, with 560 confirmed cases and 12 deaths, is categorized as a gastrointestinal system disease. It is associated with a broad range of drugs, including 7,14-dihydroxydocosa-4,8,10,12,16,19-hexaenoic acid, Abemaciclib, Adefovir dipivoxil, Amantadine, Amlodipine, Ampicillin, Axitinib, Azathioprine, and others, some of which play a role in disease pathogenesis, such as Alanine and Aspartic acid. Additionally, the condition is linked to the drug role in disease pathogenesis involving Chlorides.
200:
Zika virus has been associated with multiple outbreak events across various regions, including El Salvador, Guatemala, Ecuador, Republic of Panama, Mexico, Papua New Guinea, The Netherlands, St Lucia, United States, Viet Nam, French Guiana, India, and others, with case counts ranging from 1 to 8,750. Notable outbreaks occurred in French Guiana in November 2015 and March 2016, with 8,750 and 6 cases respectively, and in Republic of Panama in December 2015 and March 2016, with 4 and 1 cases respectively. The virus is classified as a viral infectious disease. Pharmacological agents associated with Zika virus include Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, IDX184, and Niclosamide.

Dengue Fever has been reported in numerous countries, including Brazil, Sri Lanka, India, Cameroon, Bangladesh, East Timor, Pakistan, France, Egypt, Portugal, and others, with outbreaks varying in scale from a few cases to over 80,000 cases. Notable events include a large outbreak in Sri Lanka in January 2017 with 80,732 cases and 215 deaths, and a significant outbreak in Brazil in May 2002 with 317,787 cases and 57 deaths. The disease is also categorized as a viral infectious disease. Drugs associated with Dengue Fever include 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, Doxycycline, F2-Isoprostanes, Hydroxychloroquine, Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic Acids, Rolitetracycline, Silver, cholest-5-en-3 beta,7 alpha-diol, hinokiflavone, and podocarpusflavone A. Dengue hemorrhagic fever is recognized as a subtype of Dengue Fever.

Chikungunya virus disease has been documented in countries such as United Republic of Tanzania, United States, Congo, Cameroon, France, Chad, Reunion, Italy, Spain, Argentina, and Egypt, with case numbers ranging from 1 to 27,540. A major outbreak occurred in Chad in July 2020, with 27,540 cases and 1 death. The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease. Drugs associated with Chikungunya include Chloroquine. The disease has also been reported in Italy and Spain, with multiple events recorded in 2015 and 2017.

Japanese Encephalitis has been reported in Malaysia, India, Thailand, and Australia, with outbreaks documented in 1999, 2005, 1997, and 2022. Notable events include a 1999 outbreak in Malaysia with 133 cases and 54 deaths, and a 2005 outbreak in India with 1,145 cases and 296 deaths. The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease.

Malaria has been reported in the United Republic of Tanzania, Burundi, Ethiopia, and Pakistan, with outbreaks occurring in 1998, 1999, and 2022. A significant outbreak in Pakistan in January 2022 involved 3,400,000 cases. The disease is classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. Symptoms associated with Malaria include fever, chills, vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, coma, unconsciousness, retinal hemorrhage, purpura, oliguria, pallor, pruritus, and others. Drugs associated with Malaria include Amodiaquine, Artemether, Artemisinin, Artesunate, Atovaquone, Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, Doxycycline, Quinine, Primaquine, Pyrimethamine, and various other antimalarials. The disease is linked to the Malaria pathway, and certain drugs such as Cholesterol, Glutamic acid, and bilirubin are implicated in its pathogenesis.
201: Filariasis is classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease. The drugs acetylsalicylic acid and levamisole are associated with the condition, often observed or studied in conjunction with it.
202: Cerebral malaria is classified as a form of malaria. It is associated with the use of the drugs Mefloquine and Quinine. Chagas disease, also known as American trypanosomiasis, is categorized as a type of trypanosomiasis. It is linked to several drugs including Benznidazole, Nifurtimox, Nitrofural, and other experimental compounds such as 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-methoxypropyloxy)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, and N-pip-phenylalanine-homophenylalanine-vinyl sulfone phenyl. The disease is associated with the biological pathway of Chagas disease. Parasitic infectious diseases are classified as a subset of diseases caused by infectious agents. Parasitic diseases are associated with a range of drugs, including Abamectin, Ivermectin, toosendanin, Disintegrins, and arsenamide, with Dextrose, unspecified form, playing a role in disease pathogenesis. Infections are associated with numerous drugs such as Azathioprine, Ciprofloxacin, Methotrexate, Vancomycin, and various chemotherapeutic agents including the COPP protocol, MOPP-ABV protocol, and FEC protocol, as well as immunosuppressants like Cyclosporine and Sirolimus, and antimicrobials such as Itraconazole and Metronidazole. Leigh disease is a mitochondrial metabolism disease and is associated with the oxidative phosphorylation pathway. It is linked to drugs including Methacrylyl-Coenzyme A, S-(2-carboxypropyl)cysteine, and acryloyl-coenzyme A. Ascariasis and ascaridiasis are both classified as parasitic helminthiasis infectious diseases. They are associated with drugs such as Albendazole, Mebendazole, Pyrantel pamoate, Fenbendazole, Oxibendazole, Dichlorvos, and various Chinese herbal drugs. Alzheimer’s disease is characterized by a broad range of symptoms, including amnesia, aphasia, apraxia, confusion, delirium, memory disorders, gait disturbances, hallucinations, sleep disorders, speech and language impairments, and neurobehavioral manifestations. It is associated with drugs such as Donepezil, Galantamine, Rivastigmine, Memantine, and various natural compounds including Ginkgo biloba, Melatonin, and Icariin. Certain drugs, including 4-hydroxy-2-nonenal, acrolein, aluminum, and reactive oxygen species, are implicated in disease pathogenesis, along with metabolic factors such as glutathione, hydrogen peroxide, and phosphates. The disease is associated with the Alzheimer disease pathway and is classified as a tauopathy. It shares clinical and pathological similarities with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, and Parkinson’s disease. Alzheimer’s disease 1 is considered a subtype of Alzheimer’s disease.
203:
Thrombosis is classified as a type of vascular disease and has been associated with a wide range of drugs, including anabolic agents, ancrod, arachidonic acid, azathioprine, bivalirudin, CRE 223, CX 397, carbamazepine, celecoxib, clopidogrel, oral contraceptives, contrast media, crack cocaine, cyclosporine, daltroban, dextrans, diatrizoate meglumine, doxorubicin, enzymes and coenzymes, epinephrine, estramustine, fibrinolytic agents, fondaparinux, heparin, low-molecular-weight heparin, iohexol, ioxaglic acid, iron dextran, losartan, mustard gas, nadroparin, nicotine, nisoldipine, nitroaspirin, nitrogen mustard compounds, paclitaxel, pentoxifylline, phenprocoumon, phenylephrine, procarbazine, progestins, quinapril, raloxifene hydrochloride, rofecoxib, streptozocin, tamoxifen, tranexamic acid, wortmannin, hirudin HV1, and tin protoporphyrin IX.

COVID-19, also known as coronavirus disease 2019, is associated with the biological pathway linked to coronavirus disease - COVID-19.

Hemorrhagic disease is categorized as a type of blood coagulation disease and has been associated with drugs such as busulfan, butylated hydroxytoluene, carbenicillin, cyclophosphamide, desmopressin, octreotide, phenytoin, plant resins, staphylococcal protein A, tretinoin, valproic acid, and ticarcillin-clavulanic acid.

Thrombocytopenia is classified as a blood platelet disease and is associated with drugs including acenocoumarol, acetylsalicylic acid, ajmaline, amitriptyline, antithrombins, benzylpenicillin, bleomycin, carbamazepine, cephalosporins, chromium, cimetidine, ciprofloxacin, cyclosporine, desmopressin, dioxins, doxifluridine, ELF protocol, ethambutol, flunitrazepam, fluorouracil, folic acid, fosbretabulin, heme, heparin calcium, low-molecular-weight heparin, iloprost, isoniazid, lometrexol, lomustine, M-VAC protocol, mefloquine, methylprednisolone, metoprine, mycophenolic acid, naphthalenes, ON 01910, PAVE protocol 2, propylthiouracil, ranitidine, streptozocin, thiazides, tranylcypromine, venlafaxine hydrochloride, vinblastine, zidovudine, chlorozotocin, interferons, lycium barbarum polysaccharide, s 1 (combination), and tiazofurin. Additionally, aspartic acid plays a role in the pathogenesis of thrombocytopenia, and the condition is linked to the tight junction pathway.

Blister formation has been associated with drugs such as 2-chloroethyl ethyl sulfide, 3,3-dimethylbutan-1-ol, acetylsalicylic acid, anticholinesterases, cantharidin, carbamazepine, carisoprodol, cefuroxime, chromium, colchicine, diclofenac, galantamine, glyburide, ibuprofen, indomethacin, mechlorethamine, methotrexate, mustard gas, naproxen, neostigmine, phenytoin, propylthiouracil, pyridostigmine bromide, trichloroethylene, and lewisite.

Edema has been associated with drugs including 1,2-dimethylhydrazine, 1,3-diacetoxytetradeca-6,12-dien-8,10-diyne, 2-N-carboxamidinonormianserin, (1'S, 2S)-4-amino-N-(1'-carbamoyl-2'-phenylethyl)-2-(4''-methyl-1''-naphthalenesulfonylamino)butanamide, A 967079, amlodipine, amphotericin B, cadmium chloride, chlorpropamide, cyclophosphamide, cyclosporins, cytarabine, dexamethasone, dinitrochlorobenzene, domoic acid, doxorubicin, estradiol, FK 888, fluorides, Freund's adjuvant, geraniol, gossypol, Klebsiella O3 lipopolysaccharide, melatonin, melitten, methylprednisolone, methylprednisolone aceponate, metoprolol, N-(8-amino-1-carboxyoctyl)-alanyl-proline, N-((3-(aminomethyl)phenyl)methyl)ethanimidamide, N-((4-dimethylaminophenyl)methyl)-N-(4-isopropylphenyl)-7-methoxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalen-1-carboxamide, oxazolone, phosphoramidon, prazosin, R-1,1,1-trifluoro-4-(5-fluoro-2,3-dihydrobenzofuran-7-yl)-4-methyl-2-(((2-methyl-5-quinolyl)amino)methyl)pentan-2-ol, RU24782, SC 41930, SR 48968, spironolactone, tretinoin, triamcinolone acetonide, trofosfamide, verapamil, argemone oil, canadine, chlorobromofosfamide, costunolide, deoxynivalenol, dioscin, and tris(chloroethyl)phosphate.
204: Onchocerciasis is classified as a type of filariasis. It is associated with several drugs, including Abamectin, Albendazole, Diethylcarbamazine, and Ivermectin. Filariasis, in turn, is categorized as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with the drugs Acetylsalicylic acid and Levamisole. Loiasis is also classified as a type of filariasis and is associated with the drugs Diethylcarbamazine and Ivermectin.
205:
Outbreaks of Zika virus have been reported across multiple countries between 2015 and 2021, including El Salvador, Guatemala, Ecuador, Republic of Panama, Mexico, Papua New Guinea, The Netherlands, St Lucia, United States, Viet Nam, French Guiana, India, Brazil, Dominican Republic, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Honduras, and others. Notable events include a large outbreak in French Guiana in November 2015 with 8,750 confirmed cases, and a significant outbreak in Brazil in January 2016 with 3,174 cases and 38 deaths. The virus has also been associated with cases in India, with outbreaks reported in 2016 and 2021 involving 70 and 3 cases, respectively. Zika fever is classified as a viral infectious disease. Pharmacological agents associated with Zika virus infection include Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, IDX184, and Niclosamide.

Outbreaks of Dengue Fever have occurred in numerous countries across Asia, Africa, South America, and the Caribbean, with major events reported in Sri Lanka, Brazil, India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, France, the Netherlands, and others. Notable outbreaks include a severe epidemic in Sri Lanka in January 2017 with 80,732 cases and 215 deaths, a large outbreak in Brazil in May 2002 with 317,787 cases and 57 deaths, and a significant event in Pakistan in July 2019 with 47,120 cases and 75 deaths. A particularly severe outbreak was reported in 2024 with 7,600,000 cases and 3,000 deaths, though the country was not specified. Dengue disease is classified as a viral infectious disease. Drugs associated with Dengue Fever include 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, Doxycycline, F2-Isoprostanes, Hydroxychloroquine, Hydroxyeicosatetraenoic Acids, Rolitetracycline, Silver, cholest-5-en-3 beta,7 alpha-diol, hinokiflavone, and podocarpusflavone A. Dengue hemorrhagic fever is recognized as a subtype of Dengue disease.

Outbreaks of Chikungunya virus disease have been documented in countries such as the United Republic of Tanzania, Congo, Reunion, France, Italy, Spain, Argentina, Egypt, and others. Notable events include a large outbreak in Chad in July 2020 with 27,540 cases and one death, and a significant outbreak in Reunion Island in March 2005 with 3,115 cases. The virus has also been reported in India, with an outbreak in October 2006. Chikungunya is classified as a viral infectious disease. Drugs associated with Chikungunya virus disease include Chloroquine.
206: Taeniasis is classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease. Cysticercosis is a condition that is categorized as a subtype of taeniasis. The treatment of cysticercosis involves the use of drugs including Albendazole, Dexamethasone, Praziquantel, and Steroids.
207:
Dengue Fever has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Malaysia, Sri Lanka, Egypt, The Netherlands, India, Pakistan, Brazil, and several countries in Southeast Asia, Africa, and South America, with case counts ranging from a few to over half a million and death tolls reaching several hundred. Notable outbreaks occurred in 1996, 1997, 2002, 2004, 2005, 2012, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019, 2022, and 2023, with particularly severe episodes in Sri Lanka (80,732 cases and 215 deaths in 2017), The Netherlands (over 58,000 cases and 658 deaths in 2004), and India (over 8,000 cases and 319 deaths in 1996). The disease is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with several drugs, including 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, doxycycline, hydroxychloroquine, and hinokiflavone, which are often studied or observed in conjunction with the condition. Dengue hemorrhagic fever is recognized as a subtype of dengue disease. Additionally, outbreaks have been documented in East Timor, Cameroon, Barbados, El Salvador, Cape Verde, and other locations, with varying case numbers and mortality rates.

Pneumonia has been reported in outbreaks, including one in Vietnam and Indonesia in 2003, which involved 305 confirmed cases and 5 deaths. The condition is classified as a lung disease and is associated with a range of drugs, including amikacin, erythromycin, vancomycin, meropenem, and various steroids, some of which are linked to its pathogenesis, such as arginine. It is also associated with environmental and chemical agents like asbestos, cadmium chloride, and lipopolysaccharides.
208: Malaria has been reported in multiple outbreaks across several countries, including a significant event in Burundi on May 25, 1999, which involved 616,034 confirmed cases and 51 deaths. Additional outbreaks occurred in the United Republic of Tanzania in May 1998 and July 1999, and in Ethiopia in November 2024. A major outbreak was also documented in Pakistan on January 1, 2022, with 3,400,000 confirmed cases. The disease is characterized by a range of symptoms, including fever, chills, jaundice, coma, diarrhea, vomiting, pallor, retinal hemorrhage, hemoglobinuria, and neurological manifestations such as cerebellar ataxia and unconsciousness. Malaria is classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. Several drugs are associated with its treatment or study, including Artemether, Artesunate, Chloroquine, Hydroxychloroquine, Quinine, Mefloquine, Primaquine, Atovaquone, Proguanil, and Amodiaquine. Some drugs, such as Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine, are also noted to play a role in the disease’s pathogenesis. The disease is linked to the Malaria pathway, and its pathogenesis involves biological factors such as cholesterol, glutamic acid, and bilirubin.
209:
An outbreak of Visceral Leishmaniasis occurred in Pakistan on January 16, 2002, with 738 confirmed cases, followed by two additional outbreaks on May 22 and June 28, 2002, each reporting 200,000 confirmed cases. Visceral Leishmaniasis is classified as a type of leishmaniasis, which in turn is a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. Several drugs are associated with the treatment of Visceral Leishmaniasis, including Amphotericin B, Meglumine antimoniate, Miltefosine, Pentamidine, Antimony, and various antiprotozoals. Other compounds such as Allopurinol, Artemisinin, Isosorbide dinitrate, Ketoconazole, and Thalidomide are also linked to this condition. The disease is associated with the Leishmaniasis biological pathway.

Malaria has been reported in multiple outbreaks across several countries, including Burundi in 1999 with 616,034 cases and 51 deaths, the United Republic of Tanzania in 1998 and 1999, Ethiopia in 2024, and Pakistan in 2022 with 3,400,000 confirmed cases. Malaria is also classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease. The condition is associated with a wide range of symptoms, including fever, chills, coma, unconsciousness, jaundice, diarrhea, vomiting, retinal hemorrhage, purpura, pallor, and neurological manifestations. Several drugs are associated with malaria treatment, including Chloroquine, Artemisinin, Quinine, Mefloquine, Primaquine, Pyrimethamine, Atovaquone, and various antimalarial combinations. Some drugs, such as Amodiaquine and Artesunate, are used in combination therapies. Additionally, certain compounds like Cholesterol, Glutamic acid, and Bilirubin are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis. The disease is linked to the Malaria biological pathway.
210: Sleeping sickness is classified as a type of trypanosomiasis and is associated with the biological pathway of African trypanosomiasis. Several drugs are linked to its treatment or study, including Melarsoprol, Miltefosine, Pafuramidine, Pentamidine, Phenylbutazone, Piclamilast, and octadecyl-(2-(N-methylpiperidino)ethyl)phosphate.
211: Meningitis has been associated with numerous outbreaks across multiple countries and time periods, including significant events in Burkina Faso, Nigeria, Ghana, Egypt, Chad, and the United Republic of Tanzania, with case counts ranging from dozens to over 40,000 and death tolls reaching several thousand. Notable outbreaks occurred in 1996 and 1997 in Africa, with one event in 1996 reporting 38,000 cases and 5,000 deaths, and another in 1997 involving over 40,000 cases in South Africa (KwaZulu Natal). Additional outbreaks were documented in 1999 in Egypt and 2000 in Egypt and Ethiopia, with varying case numbers and fatalities. Meningitis is classified as a central nervous system disease and is associated with a wide range of drugs, including antibiotics such as ceftriaxone, cefotaxime, ampicillin, vancomycin, and meropenem, as well as corticosteroids like dexamethasone and prednisolone, which are used to manage inflammation. It is also related to bacterial meningitis, which shares similar drug associations and is considered a subtype of meningitis. The condition is linked to the TCR signaling pathway, indicating involvement in immune response mechanisms.
212:
Cholera has been documented in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Burkina Faso, Somalia, Orissa in India, Congo, Chad, Iraq, South Africa, Zambia, Sierra Leone, Mozambique, Cameroon, Ecuador, the Republic of Panama, and the United Republic of Tanzania, with case counts ranging from a few to over 40,000 and death tolls reaching several thousand. The disease is classified as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is associated with the biological pathway of Vibrio cholerae infection. The drug cholera vaccine CVD 103-HgR is associated with the condition, indicating its use in prevention or management.

Diarrhoea has been reported in outbreaks, including one in Angola in 1998 involving 560 cases and 12 deaths. It is categorized as a gastrointestinal system disease and is linked to the pathogenesis of certain substances, including alanine and aspartic acid. A range of drugs are associated with diarrhoea, including antibiotics, anti-inflammatory agents, antipsychotics, chemotherapeutics, and various other compounds, reflecting its diverse etiology.

V. cholerae, the causative agent of cholera, has been involved in outbreaks in Cameroon, Zambia, Mexico, Niger, Congo, Sierra Leone, United Republic of Tanzania, Egypt, Philippines, Iraq, Azerbaijan, Madagascar, Comoros, and the Republic of Panama, with case numbers varying from a few to over 22,000 and fatalities reaching more than 800. The disease is associated with the biological pathway of Vibrio cholerae infection, and its pathogenesis involves the influence of substances such as chlorides.
213:
Chagas disease is classified as a type of trypanosomiasis. It is associated with several drugs, including 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxypyrrolidin-1-yl)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, 3-((2-benzyl-6-(3-methoxypropyloxy)pyridin-3-yl)ethynyl)quinuclidin-3-ol, Benznidazole, N-pip-phenylalanine-homophenylalanine-vinyl sulfone phenyl, Nifurtimox, and Nitrofural. The disease is linked to the biological pathway associated with Chagas disease.

Parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease is categorized as a type of parasitic infectious disease and is associated with a range of anthelmintic drugs, including Albendazole, Anthelmintics, Closantel, Ivermectin, Levamisole, Mebendazole, Netobimin, Niclosamide, Praziquantel, and Thiabendazole.

Megacolon is classified as a type of colonic disease and is associated with the drug Dibutylphthalate.

Strongyloidiasis is classified as a type of parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with the drugs Abamectin, Dichlorvos, Levamisole, Metrifonate, and Thiabendazole.
214: Japanese Encephalitis has been associated with multiple outbreak events across several countries, including Malaysia in 1999 with 133 confirmed cases and 54 deaths, India in 2005 with 1,145 cases and 296 deaths, and India (Andhra) in 1999 with 965 cases and 200 deaths. Additional outbreaks occurred in Thailand in 1997, reporting 247 cases and 24 deaths, and in an unspecified location in 1996 with 697 cases and 118 deaths. A more recent outbreak was documented in Australia on April 28, 2022, involving 37 confirmed cases and 3 deaths. Japanese Encephalitis is classified as a viral infectious disease.
215: Malaria has been reported in multiple outbreaks across several countries, including Tanzania in 1998 and 1999, Burundi in 1999, Pakistan in 2022 with 3.4 million confirmed cases, and Ethiopia in 2024. The disease is associated with a range of symptoms, including fever, chills, vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, coma, unconsciousness, retinal hemorrhage, purpura, oliguria, pallor, pruritus, hypergammaglobulinemia, hemoglobinuria, and neurologic manifestations. Specific forms of malaria, such as cerebral malaria and Plasmodium vivax malaria, are classified under the broader category of malaria. Treatment for malaria involves several drugs, including chloroquine, quinine, mefloquine, artemisinin, primaquine, and various drug combinations such as atovaquone-proguanil and amodiaquine-artesunate. Additional drugs associated with malaria treatment or pathogenesis include amodiaquine, artemether, artesunate, lumefantrine, pyrimethamine, sulfonamides, tetracycline, and triazoles. Some substances, such as cholesterol, bilirubin, and glutamic acid, are implicated in the disease’s pathogenesis. Malaria is also linked to the biological pathway associated with the disease itself and is categorized as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease.
216:
Dengue Fever has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including Malaysia, where significant events occurred in 1996 and 1997 with thousands of cases and several deaths. Outbreaks have also been documented in Brazil, India, the Netherlands, Cameroon, East Timor, Cape Verde, and other countries across Asia, Africa, and South America, with case counts ranging from a few to over half a million. Notable large-scale outbreaks include one in Brazil in 2002 with 317,787 cases and 57 deaths, another in the Netherlands in 2004 with over 58,000 cases and 658 deaths, and a severe outbreak in 2023 in an unspecified location with 500,000 confirmed cases and 750 deaths. A more recent outbreak in 2024 was reported with 7,600,000 cases and 3,000 deaths, indicating a significant global burden. The disease has also been observed in smaller outbreaks in countries such as Spain, Portugal, Egypt, Uruguay, and Reunion (France), with varying case numbers and mortality rates.

Dengue Fever is classified as a viral infectious disease. It is further categorized as a subtype of dengue disease, which itself is associated with dengue hemorrhagic fever. Several drugs have been observed to be associated with the disease, including 24-hydroxycholesterol, 27-hydroxycholesterol, doxycycline, F2-isoprostanes, hydroxychloroquine, hydroxyeicosatetraenoic acids, rolitetracycline, silver, cholest-5-en-3 beta,7 alpha-diol, hinokiflavone, and podocarpusflavone A. These associations may reflect either concurrent use in treatment or potential roles in disease pathogenesis.
217:
Infection is associated with a wide range of drugs, including arsenic, azathioprine, azlocillin, benzylpenicillin, bortezomib, the COPP protocol, carboplatin, cefalotin, cefapirin, cefotiam, cefoxitin, ceftazidime, ceftriaxone, cephalexin, ciprofloxacin, clarithromycin, clindamycin, cocaine, cyclophosphamide, cyclosporine, cytarabine, daunorubicin, dexamethasone, docetaxel, doxorubicin, epirubicin, etoposide, everolimus, exisulind, the FEC protocol, itraconazole, lithium, methotrexate, methylprednisolone, metronidazole, netilmicin, pemetrexed, pirarubicin, prednisone, sirolimus, the TAC protocol, tetrandrine, tobramycin, topotecan, triamcinolone, the VIP-E protocol, vancomycin, asulacrine, chlorhexidine phosphanilate, and liposomal doxorubicin.

Filariasis is classified as a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with the drugs acetylsalicylic acid and levamisole.
218:
Leprosy is classified as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is characterized by a wide range of symptoms, including ageusia, blindness, edema, eye manifestations, facial paralysis, fever, gait disorders, hyperesthesia, hypesthesia, muscle weakness, muscular atrophy, neuralgia, neurologic manifestations, oral manifestations, paralysis, paresthesia, abnormal reflexes, sensation disorders, skin manifestations, somatosensory disorders, and vision disorders. The condition is associated with several drugs, including chloroquine, clofazimine, dapsone, prednisolone, reactive oxygen species, rifampicin, thalidomide, and triptolide. Biological pathways linked to leprosy include antigen processing and presentation, cytokine-cytokine receptor interaction, natural killer cell mediated cytotoxicity, protein processing in the endoplasmic reticulum, and Toll-like receptor signaling pathway.

Lepromatous leprosy, a subtype of leprosy, shares the same drug associations as leprosy, including clofazimine, dapsone, ethionamide, ofloxacin, prednisolone, prednisone, rifampicin, and thalidomide.

Tuberculosis is also categorized as a primary bacterial infectious disease and is associated with a broad spectrum of drugs, including amikacin, aminosalicylic acid, amithiozone, bedaquiline, capreomycin, chenodeoxycholic acid, ciprofloxacin, cycloserine, ethambutol, ethionamide, gentamicins, iron, isoniazid, kanamycin, menaquinone, OPC-67683, ofloxacin, pitolisant, prednisolone, pyrazinamide, pyridoxine, pyrroles, rifampicin, streptomycin, terizidone, tretinoin, verapamil, viomycin, isofon, and the combination isoniazid/pyrazinamide/rifampin. Certain drugs, such as lopinavir, phosphorus, and phosphates, are implicated in the disease's pathogenesis. The condition is associated with the tuberculosis pathway.

Infarction is linked to a variety of drugs, including acetylsalicylic acid, anticoagulants, atenolol, CP 31398, cocaine, contraceptive agents, contraceptives, oral, hormonal contraceptives, dexfenfluramine, doxorubicin, ergotamine, ethyl pyruvate, fibrinolytic agents, heparin, isoprenaline, mitomycin, papaverine, quercetin, rizatriptan, tranexamic acid, zolmitriptan, and corticosteroids. Additionally, malonaldehyde and silver are involved in the pathogenesis of infarction.

Furunculosis is associated with the drug bacampicillin.
219: Malaria has been reported in multiple outbreaks across various regions, including a significant event in Burundi on May 25, 1999, which involved 616,034 confirmed cases and 51 deaths. Additional outbreaks occurred in the United Republic of Tanzania in May 1998 and July 1999, as well as in Pakistan on January 1, 2022, with 3,400,000 confirmed cases, and in Ethiopia on November 1, 2024. A separate outbreak of Plasmodium was reported in Barbados on February 9, 2007, with 280 confirmed cases. Malaria is classified as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease and is associated with the pathogen Plasmodium malariae. The disease presents with a wide range of symptoms, including fever, chills, jaundice, coma, unconsciousness, diarrhea, vomiting, retinal and eye hemorrhage, pallor, purpura, cerebellar ataxia, neurologic manifestations, oliguria, hemoglobinuria, hypergammaglobulinemia, and fetal hypoxia. Several drugs are associated with malaria treatment or pathogenesis, including amodiaquine, artemether, artesunate, chloroquine, hydroxychloroquine, quinine, mefloquine, atovaquone-proguanil combination, and sulfonamides. Some drugs, such as chloroquine diphosphate and desmethylastemizole, are noted for their role in disease pathogenesis, while others like cholesterol, bilirubin, and glutamic acid are implicated in the disease’s biological mechanisms. The condition is linked to the Malaria pathway.
220:
Parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease is classified as a parasitic infectious disease and is also categorized as a type of parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease. It is associated with a range of drugs including Albendazole, Anthelmintics, Closantel, Ivermectin, Levamisole, Mebendazole, Netobimin, Niclosamide, Praziquantel, and Thiabendazole. 

Lung disease is a lower respiratory tract disease and is associated with numerous drugs such as 1,4-dioxane, 1-nitronaphthalene, 2-chloroethyl ethyl sulfide, various acetic acid derivatives, Amiodarone, antigens from Dermatophagoides and nuclear sources, arsenic compounds, barium, carbon, Carmustine, Cisplatin, Co-Trimoxazole, Cyclophosphamide, Cytarabine, Deferoxamine, Dexamethasone, Dobutamine, Docetaxel, Etoposide, Fiacitabine, Fluconazole, Formaldehyde, Grape Seed Proanthocyanidins, Hesperidin, Isotretinoin, Lipopolysaccharides, Mechlorethamine, Metamfetamine, Methacholine chloride, Minocycline, Mitomycin, Nickel, Nicotine, Pimagedine, Roxithromycin, Sulfasalazine, Thiotepa, Tretinoin, Vindesine, Vinorelbine, Vitamin A, Volatile Organic Compounds, Warfarin, beta-1,3-glucan, nickel monoxide, polyhexamethyleneguanidine, thieno(2,3-c)isoquinolin-5-one, and vanadium pentoxide.

Opisthorchiasis is a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is associated with drugs such as Anthocyanins, Praziquantel, Reactive Nitrogen Species, Reactive Oxygen Species, and Turmeric.

Diabetes mellitus is a glucose metabolism disease and is associated with drugs including 2,4,5-Trichlorophenoxyacetic Acid, Acetylsalicylic acid, Aldrin, Atrazine, Bezafibrate, Bisphenol A, Blood Glucose, Chlorpyrifos, Chromium, Diazoxide, Flavonoids, Gliclazide, Heptachlor, Iron, Metals, Heavy, Metrifonate, Netoglitazone, Omega-3 fatty acids, Particulate Matter, Pentamidine, Perindopril, Phenformin, Plant Extracts, Plant Preparations, Potassium, Pravastatin, Raloxifene Hydrochloride, Repaglinide, Rosiglitazone, Streptozocin, Tacrolimus, Telmisartan, Thiazides, Thioridazine, Triethylenetetramine, Trifluoperazine, Uranium, Vanadium Compounds, Voglibose, alachlor, aminoacetone, aroclor 1260, arsenite, cyanazine, lipoxin A4, methylarsonite, nimbidiol, osthol, pregnadiene-3,16-dione, and terbufos. It is also linked to the role of certain substances in disease pathogenesis, including Alanine, chemokine cx3cl1, and pyrimidine.

Infection is associated with drugs such as Arsenic, Azathioprine, Benzylpenicillin, Bortezomib, COPP protocol, Carboplatin, Cefalotin, Cefapirin, Cefotiam, Cefoxitin, Ceftazidime, Ceftriaxone, Cephalexin, Ciprofloxacin, Clarithromycin, Clindamycin, Cocaine, Cyclophosphamide, Cyclosporine, Cytarabine, Daunorubicin, Desoxycortone, Dexamethasone, Docetaxel, Etoposide, Everolimus, Exisulind, Fludarabine, Itraconazole, LNH 87 protocol, Lithium, MOPP-ABV protocol, Melphalan, Methotrexate, Methylprednisolone, Metronidazole, Netilmicin, Pemetrexed, Prednisone, Silver sulfadiazine, Sirolimus, TAC protocol, Tetrandrine, Tobramycin, Topotecan, Triamcinolone, VIP-E protocol, asulacrine, chlorhexidine phosphanilate, and liposomal doxorubicin.

HIV infection is associated with the biological pathway of Human immunodeficiency virus 1 infection.

Gastric ulcer is a peptic ulcer disease and is associated with drugs such as 2-Ethoxyethanol, 2-phenylquinoline, Acetylcysteine, Acetylsalicylic acid, Alcohols, Ampicillin, Butoxamine, Camostat, Chlorpheniramine, Cocaine, Crack Cocaine, Diclofenac, Gallic Acid, Glutathione, Ketanserin, Lemon oil, Leukotriene D4, Limonene, Lupeol, Menthol, N(1)-methylnicotinamide, N-(2-cyclohexyloxy-4-nitrophenyl)methanesulfonamide, Omeprazole, Oxazepam, Oxygen, PD 136450, Phenacetin, Phenylbutazone, Phosphoramidon, Plastoquinone, R 97, Rabeprazole, Rebamipide, Reserpine, Silymarin, Sodium nitrate, Sulfapyridine, Sulpiride, Thalidomide, Tianeptine, Zinc, chebulagic acid, chelerythrine, costunolide, fenflumizole, fucoidan, lipopolysaccharide from Helicobacter pylori, prostaglandins, thiaton, and trichorabdal A. It is also linked to the role of Rabeprazole in disease pathogenesis.

Stomach cancer is a gastrointestinal system cancer and resembles uterine fibroid. It presents with symptoms including acute abdomen, abdominal pain, anorexia, asthenia, ataxia telangiectasia, body weight changes, cachexia, constipation, diarrhea, dyspepsia, edema, emaciation, eructation, fatigue, fever, flatulence, gastroparesis, heartburn, hematemesis, hiccup, hyperemesis gravidarum, ideal body weight, obstructive jaundice, nausea, postoperative pain, purpura, thrombocytopenic, idiopathic purpura, thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura, vomiting, anticipatory vomiting, and weight loss. It is associated with drugs such as 1,2,5,6-dibenzanthracene, 1,3-dichloro-1-propene, 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-nitrosourea, 1-amino-2,4-dibromoanthraquinone, 2-amino-3,4-dimethylimidazo(4,5-f)quinoline, 2-amino-3-methylimidazo(4,5-f)quinoline, 5-Amino-3-((5-nitro-2-furyl)vinyl)-1,2,4-oxadiazole, 5-FU-CDDP protocol, Acetaldehyde, Acrylonitrile, Air Pollutants, Ascorbic acid, Benzo(a)pyrene, Butylated hydroxyanisole, Capecitabine, Celecoxib, Cimetidine, Crizotinib, Diethylnitrosamine, Docosahexaenoic Acids, Drugs, Chinese Herbal, Ethanol, Famotidine, Fluorouracil, Folic acid, Fosbretabulin, Imatinib Mesylate, Indomethacin, Irinotecan, Lapatinib, Methotrexate, Methylnitronitrosoguanidine, Methylnitrosourea, Norepinephrine, Paclitaxel, Sodium Chloride, Dietary, Sodium chloride, Sulfasalazine, Tegafur, Trastuzumab emtansine, Troglitazone, XELOX, benzyl acetate, chlorophyll, folpet, hmg coa reductase inhibitors, loxtidine, methyl bromide, oxiplatin, and ptaquiloside. It is linked to the pathway of Gastric cancer.

Stomach carcinoma is a type of stomach cancer.

Rheumatoid arthritis is an arthritis and resembles systemic lupus erythematosus and systemic scleroderma. It presents with symptoms including Adie Syndrome, arthralgia, ataxia, back pain, cachexia, chorea, chronic pain, dyspepsia, edema, eye manifestations, facial pain, fatigue, fever, fever of unknown origin, hearing loss, conductive and sensorineural, heartburn, hemiplegia, hoarseness, hyperalgesia, hypergammaglobulinemia, metatarsalgia, monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance, muscular atrophy, nausea, neck pain, neuralgia, neurologic manifestations, oral manifestations, pain, pain threshold, postoperative pain, paralysis, paresthesia, phantom limb, proteinuria, psychophysiologic disorders, purpura, hyperglobulinemic purpura, Schoenlein-Henoch purpura, thrombocytopenic purpura, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura, quadriplegia, Schnitzler Syndrome, sciatica, sensation disorders, shoulder pain, skin manifestations, taste disorders, and tonic pupil. It is associated with drugs such as Acetylsalicylic acid, Alclofenac, Antigens, Auranofin, Aurothioglucose, Chlorambucil, Chlormezanone, Curcumin, Cyclosporine, Cyclosporins, Dexamethasone, Dinoprostone, Entinostat, Famotidine, Fenclofenac, Fenoprofen, Flurbiprofen, Freund's Adjuvant, GW 506033X, Glyphosate, Gold Compounds, Hydrocortisone, Ketoprofen, Mefenamic acid, Meloxicam, Methylprednisolone hemisuccinate, Nabumetone, Organogold Compounds, Ozone, Penicillamine, Pirprofen, Plant Extracts, Prospidium, Resveratrol, Rifamycin, Salicylates, Sodium aurothiomalate, Sulfasalazine, Sulindac, Tacrolimus, Tenoxicam, Tiopronin, Tobacco Smoke Pollution, Tofacitinib, Valdecoxib, Zhengqing Fengtongning, lobenzarit, mancozeb, pesticides, and thymoquinone. It is also linked to the role of Calcifediol and Pyrrole-2-Carboxylate in disease pathogenesis, and is associated with the pathway of Rheumatoid arthritis.

Cancer is a disease of cellular proliferation and is associated with drugs such as 2,4,5,2',4',5'-hexachlorobiphenyl, 2-chloroethyl-3-sarcosinamide-1-nitrosourea, 2-methoxyestradiol-3,17-O,O-bis(sulfamate), 2-methoxyoestra-1,3,5(10),16-tetraene-3-carboxamide, Ascorbic acid, Berberine, Cadmium Chloride, Carbon, Carmustine, Catechols, Cisplatin, Cobalt, Conjugated estrogens, Daunorubicin, EPTC, Epoprostenol, Fluorouracil, Methylnitrosourea, N-(2-cyclohexyloxy-4-nitrophenyl)methanesulfonamide, Niclosamide, PC protocol, Pantothenic acid, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons, Potassium, Prasugrel Hydrochloride, Procarbazine, Salinomycin, Soot, TDP 521252, Terameprocol, Urethane, VX680, Valproic acid, Vitamin A, Vitamin E, Water Pollutants, Chemical, acylfulvene, asulacrine, azinomycin epoxide, chlorobromofosfamide, chromium hexavalent ion, cinnabarinic acid, cucurbitacin I, cyanoginosin LR, fullerene C60, glucocorticoids, methanesulfonamide, nickel subsulfide, sanguinarine, and usnic acid. It is also linked to the role of Amines, Glycine, Kynurenic Acid, ethyl arachidonate, and trimethionine in disease pathogenesis.

Gastritis is a stomach disease and is associated with drugs such as Acetaminophen, Acetylsalicylic acid, Alendronate, Ammonia, Amoxicillin, Anti-Inflammatory Agents, Non-Steroidal, Bezafibrate, Cimetidine, Diazepam, Diclofenac, Dicyclomine, Diethylnitrosamine, Diquat, Drugs, Chinese Herbal, Ethanol, Famotidine, Folic acid, Hydrochloric acid, Ibuprofen, Indomethacin, Insecticides, Iodoacetamide, Irbesartan, JP8 aviation fuel, Ketamine, Ketoprofen, Lansoprazole, Metoclopramide, Misoprostol, Octreotide, Omeprazole, Ondansetron, Oxyphenbutazone, Pantoprazole, Phenol, Piroxicam, Plant Preparations, Praziquantel, Prochlorperazine, Rabeprazole, Saccharin, Ticlopidine, Zinc, cefuroxime axetil, glucocorticoids, isosakuranetin, jinghua weikang, poncirin, sangre de grado, and trichorabdal A.

Parasitic infectious disease is a disease by infectious agent.

Parasitic Diseases are associated with drugs such as Abamectin, Arsenamide, Disintegrins, Ivermectin, and toosendanin, and are linked to the role of Dextrose, unspecified form, in disease pathogenesis.

Colorectal cancer is a large intestine cancer and is associated with drugs such as 1,2-Dimethylhydrazine, 2-Amino-1-methyl-6-phenylimidazo(4,5-b)pyridine, 2-amino-1-methyl-6-phenolimidazo(4,5-b)pyridine-DNA adduct, 2-amino-3,8-dimethylimidazo(4,5-f)quinoxaline, 4-acetylantroquinonol B, 4-biphenylamine, Acifluorfen, Acrolein, Andrographolide, Anti-Inflammatory Agents, Non-Steroidal, Arsenic, Arsenic trioxide, Azoxymethane, Bromoform, Carmustine, Cisplatin, Copper, Decitabine, Dietary Fats, Doxifluridine, Epigallocatechin gallate, Erlotinib Hydrochloride, Etodolac, Fenretinide, Folfox protocol, Fonofos, Gefitinib, Gemcitabine, Hemin, Irinotecan, Leucovorin, Levamisole, Magnesium, Mesalazine, Methotrexate, Mitomycin, Niclosamide, Organoplatinum Compounds, Polyamines, Polychlorinated Biphenyls, Pyridoxal phosphate, Quercetin, Riboflavin, Thymidine, Vinblastine, Vitamin E, XELOX, Zidovudine, oxiplatin, and s 1 (combination). It is linked to the pathways of Colorectal cancer, MicroRNAs in cancer, and O-linked glycosylation.

Colorectal carcinoma is a type of colorectal cancer.
221: Sleeping sickness is classified as a type of trypanosomiasis and is associated with the biological pathway of African trypanosomiasis. The condition is linked to several drugs, including Melarsoprol, Miltefosine, Pafuramidine, Pentamidine, Phenylbutazone, Piclamilast, and octadecyl-(2-(N-methylpiperidino)ethyl)phosphate, which are used in its treatment or study. Trypanosomiasis, in turn, is categorized as a parasitic protozoa infectious disease and is associated with additional drugs such as Antimony, Arsenic compounds, N,N'-diphenyl-4-methoxybenzamidine, Pentamidine, and borrelidin.
222:
An outbreak of HIV infection-AIDS occurred in Pakistan on April 25, 2019, with 876 confirmed cases. HIV infection is classified as a viral infectious disease and is associated with the biological pathway of Human immunodeficiency virus 1 infection. The condition is linked to various drugs, including Adefovir dipivoxil, Atazanavir Sulfate, Efavirenz, Lamivudine, Lopinavir, Maraviroc, Ritonavir, Tenofovir, Zidovudine, and others, which are used in its treatment or studied in relation to its pathogenesis. Additionally, HIV infection is associated with opportunistic infections, which are themselves classified as bacterial infectious diseases, and these are managed with drugs such as Dexamethasone and Temozolomide.

Schistosomiasis is a parasitic helminthiasis infectious disease and is further categorized as a schistosomiasis condition. It is associated with several drugs, including Metrifonate, Praziquantel, and others such as Quercetin and Resveratrol for intestinal schistosomiasis. The infection caused by Schistosoma mansonii is linked to a broader range of drugs, including Acetylcysteine, Artemether, Oxamniquine, and 3,7-dimethyloctan-1-ol, with triglycerides playing a role in disease pathogenesis. Other parasitic helminthiasis infectious diseases are treated with drugs like Albendazole, Ivermectin, Mebendazole, and Praziquantel. Viral infectious diseases, including HIV infection, are also associated with antiviral agents such as Tilorone, Cidofovir, and Melatonin.
223:
Epilepsy is associated with a wide range of symptoms, including seizures, amnesia, aphasia, ataxia, myoclonus, coma, sleep disorders, intellectual disability, and various forms of neurological dysfunction such as cortical blindness, spasticity, and consciousness disturbances.
